title: Closure of Strait of Hormuz is 'greatest global energy security threat in history,' warns IEA chief
subhed: Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency, does not rule out another release of part of the member states' strategic oil reserves to ease prices.

title: White House says it could 'take out' Iran's Kharg island anytime

US jury finds Elon Musk misled Twitter shareholders
subhed: According to damages calculated by jurors, Musk could be ordered to pay out as much as $2.6 billion. Musk abandoned his effort to get out of buying Twitter in late 2022 after the company took him to court to uphold the contract. The tech billionaire has since merged the social media platform – now called X – with SpaceX and his startup xAI.

title: 'With Operation Epic Fury, the US turns pop culture into the language of Pentagon violence'
subhed: In this op-ed for Le Monde, Louis Lapeyrie, a researcher at Paris-I Panthéon-Sorbonne University, examines the visual and narrative shifts in military communications under the Trump administration and explores the many borrowings from video games.

title: Chuck Norris, king of martial arts and Reagan-era action movies, has died
subhed: The American actor best known for the hit series 'Walker, Texas Ranger' died on Friday, at the age of 86.

title: Richard Ford: 'The regime that most urgently needs changing is the one that sits in Washington'
subhed: 'Writers on the war in the Middle East' 1/4. Le Monde asked writers Richard Ford, Zeruya Shalev and Navid Kermani for their perspectives on the war.

Putin conducts a 'special musical operation' in Europe
subhed: Barred from the West, Valery Gergiev, maestro at the helm of Russia's two leading opera houses and cultural ambassador for Vladimir Putin, is seeking a return to conducting in Europe's major concert halls. In this cultural arena, Moscow is shifting from 'soft power' to 'sharp power.'

title: How Budapest became a hub for Western nationalists under Viktor Orban
subhed: The Hungarian capital, popular among hard-right circles in the West, is home to a number of think tanks and conferences promoting the image of Prime Minister Viktor Orban with the approach of the legislative elections on April 12.

title: In Paris, Reza Pahlavi, son of Iran's last shah, builds his network
subhed: Reza Pahlavi has been based in the French capital since the US-Israeli strikes on Iran started, making moves in anticipation of the Islamic Republic's fall.
